commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Maintainer review is needed to accept the upgrade-visible browser-local session reset; no narrow automated repair remains.; Cleared: Cleared; the diff is limited to browser-local settings restore logic and UI tests, with no dependency, workflow, secret, permission, or production code-execution surface. (sha=2d718489a7da952b7188d3843e13d0e5a0ee5f2e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR resets legacy top-level routed Control UI session keys to `main`, preserves scoped `sessionsByGateway` selections, and adds storage/browser regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +61, Tests +295. Total +356 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main and v2026.6.9 return legacy top-level routed `sessionKey` values unchanged from `loadSettings()`, while the PR adds focused storage and browser coverage for the after behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review is needed to accept the upgrade-visible browser-local session reset; no narrow automated repair remains.

target.title: fix(ui): reset routed sessions to main on restore
target.body:
Closes #61661

## Summary

- Reset legacy top-level routed Control UI session keys to `main` during restore so reload does not reopen stale historical routed sessions.
- Preserve valid scoped `sessionsByGateway` routed selections for the active gateway instead of flattening every routed restore back to `main`.
- Keep the behavior change confined to Control UI restore sanitization and regression coverage; live gateway routing and backend session semantics stay unchanged.
- Intentionally out of scope: changing live session routing behavior after connection or redefining the broader routed-session persistence policy beyond this restore-layer fix.
- Success looks like legacy top-level routed values reopening on `main`, scoped per-gateway routed values staying sticky, and reviewers having explicit proof for the compatibility-sensitive reset.
- Reviewers should focus on the restore classifier in `ui/src/ui/storage.ts` plus the focused storage/browser regression coverage.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #61661

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #61661

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; this PR is the linked implementation candidate for the canonical routed-session restore report.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI could reload into a stale historical routed session from older persisted top-level settings instead of reopening on the real `main` session, while valid scoped per-gateway routed selections still needed to survive reload.
- Real environment tested: isolated temporary OpenClaw gateway plus a real Control UI browser session on `pi-pr-lab` / piclaw for branch proof commit `afa2413e82`; current head `2d718489a7da952b7188d3843e13d0e5a0ee5f2e` is a readability-only follow-up in the same restore helper and preserves the proved restore behavior.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Started an isolated temporary OpenClaw gateway for the PR branch.
  2. Seeded Control UI storage with the historical routed direct/thread key `agent:main:telegram:direct:12345:thread:12345:99`.
  3. Loaded the real Control UI in a browser and connected it to the temporary gateway.
  4. Repeated the reload flow with a scoped `sessionsByGateway` routed key for the same gateway.
  5. Revalidated current head `2d718489a7` with `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`, `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental false --pretty false`, `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s lint`, `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`, and `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live browser proof already recorded on this PR shows `OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (f49955f)` running in an isolated temp gateway, successful RPCs `connect`, `sessions.subscribe`, `agents.list`, and `chat.history`, the UI restoring to `/chat?session=main`, the rendered page showing the main Chat session, and the historical routed key absent from the rendered DOM after restore. The same branch also carries browser-level Control UI E2E coverage in `ui/src/ui/e2e/session-restore.e2e.test.ts` for both the legacy top-level reset path and the scoped per-gateway preservation path.
- Observed result after fix: legacy top-level routed session values reopen on `main` instead of reviving a stale routed session, scoped per-gateway routed selections still restore for the active gateway, and the current exact head is back to `status: 👀 ready for maintainer look` / `proof: sufficient` after the proof-capable review.
- What was not tested: no separate fresh manual browser run was repeated after the readability-only `2d718489a7` follow-up because that change only replaced sparse destructuring with explicit indexed locals inside `isHistoricalRoutedSessionKey`; no live third-party production gateway/account was used.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this worker container cannot launch local Chromium because `libglib-2.0.so.0` is missing, so the durable real-browser proof comes from the isolated `pi-pr-lab` run already attached to the PR history and the current head is validated locally with focused UI checks plus a clean merge-tree.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this fix, current main and the latest release line could restore the stored top-level routed `sessionKey` unchanged, which reopened a stale routed session after reload instead of returning the UI to `main`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s lint`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added browser-level Control UI reload coverage in `ui/src/ui/e2e/session-restore.e2e.test.ts` for both the legacy top-level routed-key reset and scoped per-gateway routed-key preservation paths.
- Expanded `ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts` coverage for valid and invalid historical routed restore shapes.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Before the restore fix, top-level persisted routed session keys could survive reload unchanged and reopen the UI on a stale routed session instead of `main`.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

What is the highest-risk area?

- Compatibility-sensitive Control UI restore behavior for existing browser-local settings that still contain legacy top-level routed session keys.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The classifier change is confined to restore-time sanitization, valid scoped per-gateway routed selections are preserved explicitly, and both storage-level and browser-level regression coverage exercise the affected reload paths.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review on current head `2d718489a7da952b7188d3843e13d0e5a0ee5f2e`; the missing `Real behavior proof` section has been restored with the existing branch proof and current-head validation, so no additional scoped code change is pending.
target.author: stevenepalmer
